Summary: TC-01: Successful policy lookup
Description:
Scenario: Successful policy lookup
  Given I am on the Policy Lookup page
  When I enter policy number "POL-AB123"
  And I click Submit
  Then I should see the policy details with status "ACTIVE"
  And I should see the coverage grid with coverage types and limits

Summary: TC-02: Policy number not found
Description:
Scenario: Policy number not found
  Given I am on the Policy Lookup page
  When I enter policy number "POL-XXXXXX"
  And I click Submit
  Then I should see an error message "Policy not found"
  And the policy details card should not be displayed

Summary: TC-03: Invalid policy number format
Description:
Scenario: Invalid policy number format
  Given I am on the Policy Lookup page
  When I enter "invalid-number"
  And I click Submit
  Then I should see a validation error before any API call is made
  And the error should indicate the expected format

Summary: TC-04: Successful claim submission
Description:
Scenario: Successful claim submission with all valid data
  Given I am logged in as a CUSTOMER
  And policy "POL-AB123" is ACTIVE with MEDICAL coverage limit of 10000
  When I submit a claim with:
    | policyNumber   | POL-AB123             |
    | claimType      | MEDICAL               |
    | claimAmount    | 1500.00               |
    | incidentDate   | 2026-03-01            |
    | description    | Medical treatment after workplace accident |
  Then the response status should be 201 Created
  And the claim status should be "SUBMITTED"
  And a ClaimHistory entry should exist with status SUBMITTED

Summary: TC-05: Claim submission with non-existent policy
Description:
Scenario: Claim submission with non-existent policy number
  Given I am logged in as a CUSTOMER
  When I submit a claim with policy number "POL-XXXXXX"
  Then the response status should be 404 Not Found
  And the error message should contain "Policy not found"

Summary: TC-06: Claim submission with expired policy
Description:
Scenario: Claim submission against expired policy
  Given policy "POL-EF789" has status EXPIRED
  When I submit a claim for policy "POL-EF789"
  Then the response status should be 422 Unprocessable Entity
  And the error message should contain "Policy is not active"

Summary: TC-07: Claim amount exceeds coverage limit
Description:
Scenario: Claim amount exceeds coverage limit
  Given policy "POL-AB123" has MEDICAL coverage with limit 10000
  When I submit a MEDICAL claim for 10001
  Then the response status should be 422 Unprocessable Entity
  And the error message should contain "Claim amount exceeds coverage limit"

Summary: TC-08: Claim with inactive coverage
Description:
Scenario: Claim for inactive coverage type
  Given policy "POL-AB123" has VISION coverage with is_active = false
  When I submit a VISION claim
  Then the response status should be 422 Unprocessable Entity
  And the error message should contain "Coverage is not active"

Summary: TC-09: Claim submission with zero amount
Description:
Scenario: Claim submission with zero amount
  When I submit a claim with claimAmount = 0
  Then the response status should be 400 Bad Request

Summary: TC-10: Claim submission with short description
Description:
Scenario: Claim submission with description too short
  When I submit a claim with a 5-character description
  Then the response status should be 400 Bad Request

Summary: TC-11: Claim type not covered by policy
Description:
Scenario: Claim type has no matching coverage on policy
  Given policy "POL-CD456" has only MEDICAL and DENTAL coverages
  When I submit a HOME claim against policy "POL-CD456"
  Then the response status should be 422 Unprocessable Entity
  And the error message should contain "Coverage type not found"

Summary: TC-12: Dupilcate claim within 24 hours
Description:
Scenario: Duplicate claim within 24 hours is rejected
  Given I submitted a MEDICAL claim for policy "POL-AB123" at T=0
  When I submit another MEDICAL claim for policy "POL-AB123" at T+1 hour
  Then the response status should be 409 Conflict
  And the error message should indicate a duplicate submission

Summary: TC-13: Claim after 24 hours allowed
Description:
Scenario: Claim submitted 25 hours after previous is accepted
  Given I submitted a MEDICAL claim for policy "POL-AB123" at T=0
  When I submit another MEDICAL claim for policy "POL-AB123" at T+25 hours
  Then the response status should be 201 Created

Summary: TC-14: Different type not blocked
Description:
Scenario: Different claim type is not subject to duplicate block
  Given I submitted a MEDICAL claim for policy "POL-AB123" at T=0
  When I submit a DENTAL claim for policy "POL-AB123" at T+1 hour
  Then the response status should be 201 Created

Summary: TC-15: Exact 24-hour boundary
Description:
Scenario: Claim at exactly 24h boundary
  Given I submitted a claim at T=0
  When I submit the same type at T+24 hours exactly
  Then the response status should be 409 Conflict

Summary: TC-16: View SUBMITTED claim status
Description:
Scenario: Customer views a SUBMITTED claim status
  Given claim 1 has status SUBMITTED
  When I navigate to claim 1 status page
  Then I see a blue SUBMITTED badge
  And I see the claim type and amount

Summary: TC-17: View APPROVED claim status
Description:
Scenario: Customer views an APPROVED claim status
  Given claim 3 has status APPROVED
  When I navigate to claim 3 status page
  Then I see a green APPROVED badge

Summary: TC-18: View REJECTED claim status
Description:
Scenario: Customer views a REJECTED claim status
  Given claim 4 has status REJECTED
  When I navigate to claim 4 status page
  Then I see a red REJECTED badge

Summary: TC-19: Claim history shows all transitions
Description:
Scenario: Claim history shows full status transition chain
  Given claim 3 was: SUBMITTED then IN_REVIEW then APPROVED
  When I navigate to claim 3 history page
  Then I see 3 history entries
  And the entries are sorted oldest to newest

Summary: TC-20: Reviewer notes visible in history
Description:
Scenario: Reviewer notes are shown on admin actions
  Given claim 3 has an APPROVED history entry with notes "All documentation verified"
  When I view claim 3 history
  Then I see the reviewer notes on the APPROVED entry

Summary: TC-21: Policy claims list shows all statuses
Description:
Scenario: Policy claims list shows all claim statuses
  Given policy "POL-AB123" has claims in SUBMITTED, IN_REVIEW, APPROVED, and REJECTED
  When I navigate to the policy claims list
  Then I see claims in all four statuses

Summary: TC-22: Admin can approve SUBMITTED claim
Description:
Scenario: Admin approves a SUBMITTED claim
  Given claim 1 has status SUBMITTED
  When admin clicks Approve with notes "Claim verified"
  Then the claim status changes to APPROVED
  And a new history entry with APPROVED is created

Summary: TC-23: Admin can approve IN_REVIEW claim
Description:
Scenario: Admin approves an IN_REVIEW claim
  Given claim 2 has status IN_REVIEW
  When admin clicks Approve
  Then the claim status changes to APPROVED

Summary: TC-24: Cannot approve already APPROVED claim
Description:
Scenario: Double approval is prevented
  Given claim 3 has status APPROVED
  When admin attempts to approve claim 3 again
  Then the response status should be 409 Conflict

Summary: TC-25: Admin can reject with notes
Description:
Scenario: Admin rejects a claim with mandatory notes
  Given claim 1 has status SUBMITTED
  When admin clicks Reject with notes "Pre-existing condition not covered"
  Then the claim status changes to REJECTED
  And a history entry is created with the rejection notes

Summary: TC-26: Reject requires notes
Description:
Scenario: Rejection without notes is rejected
  Given claim 1 has status SUBMITTED
  When admin clicks Reject without entering any notes
  Then a validation error is shown on the notes field
  And the API is not called

Summary: TC-27: Cannot reject already REJECTED claim
Description:
Scenario: Double rejection is prevented
  Given claim 4 has status REJECTED
  When admin attempts to reject claim 4 again
  Then the response status should be 409 Conflict

Summary: TC-28: Admin views audit trail
Description:
Scenario: Admin views complete audit trail for claim 3
  Given claim 3 has 3 history entries
  When admin navigates to claim 3 detail page
  Then all 3 history entries are displayed
  And entries are sorted chronologically
  And reviewer notes are visible on the APPROVED entry

Summary: TC-29: CUSTOMER cannot access admin route
Description:
Scenario: Customer role cannot access admin pages
  Given I am logged in as CUSTOMER
  When I navigate to /admin/claims
  Then I should be redirected to an Unauthorised page

Summary: TC-30: Unauthenticated redirect to login
Description:
Scenario: Unauthenticated user is redirected
  Given I am not logged in
  When I navigate to /dashboard
  Then I should be redirected to /login
